g.data-narration: Welcome to 'About The Board' - an in-depth exploration of IoT boards and their hardware components.
g.text: About The Board: Understanding IoT Hardware

g.data-narration: IoT boards are specialized single-board computers designed to connect physical devices to the digital world.
Understanding IoT board architecture is essential for connecting sensors correctly and preventing hardware damage.
g.text: • Prevents hardware damage from wrong connections
• Essential for home automation and sensor projects

g.data-narration: Every IoT board contains four essential building blocks: processor, memory, interfaces, and communication modules.
Microcontrollers are self-contained chips for dedicated tasks, while microprocessors need external components and run full operating systems.
g.text: Single-chip, low-power, dedicated tasks • Arduino, ESP32
External memory, runs OS • Raspberry Pi, Intel Edison

g.data-narration: IoT boards use three main memory types: RAM for temporary data, ROM for permanent instructions, and Flash for program storage.
Four key interfaces connect sensors and actuators: GPIO for digital I/O, UART for serial, SPI for high-speed, and I2C for multi-device.
Communication modules enable connectivity: Wi-Fi for wireless networking, Bluetooth for short-range, Ethernet for wired, and cellular for mobile.
Arduino boards are beginner-friendly, use simple programming, and are perfect for prototyping and hobbyist IoT projects.
g.text: Easy to learn • Simple IDE • 5V logic

g.data-narration: Raspberry Pi offers full Linux OS, HDMI output, USB ports, and powerful processing for advanced IoT applications and gateways.
g.text: Full Linux OS • HDMI • USB • Wi-Fi

g.data-narration: ESP32 and ESP8266 provide built-in Wi-Fi and Bluetooth at low cost, ideal for wireless IoT applications requiring connectivity.
g.text: Perfect for: Wireless sensors, Home Auto

g.data-narration: Intel Edison and Galileo offer high computing power for edge analytics and industrial IoT applications requiring advanced processing.
g.text: High compute • x86 compatibility • Yocto Linux

g.data-narration: This comparison helps you choose the right board based on processing power, connectivity, cost, and ideal applications.
IoT boards power real-world applications including smart homes, industrial monitoring, wearables, and environmental sensing systems.
In summary, IoT boards are essential for connecting the physical and digital worlds, with each board type suited for different applications.
g.text: ✓ MCU vs MPU: Choose based on processing needs
✓ Interfaces: GPIO, UART, SPI, I2C for sensors
